mnemonics: SEE Data Clearly: Summarize, Examine, Evaluate
keyPoints: Use descriptive statistics (mean, median, standard deviation) to summarize continuous data.
Visualize data distributions using histograms, box plots, and scatter plots to identify patterns and outliers.
Check for assumptions of statistical models, such as normality and homoscedasticity, which are crucial for valid CIE results.
commonMistakes: Ignoring outliers or not investigating their cause, which can skew the analysis.
Using inappropriate measures of central tendency for skewed distributions (e.g., using mean instead of median).
Failing to check for data quality issues like missing values or incorrect data entry before analysis.
